reviewer.displayName: VSBDev — accountable operator, academic review role

findings.finding: The objective promises recovery of the exponent and proportionality constant from exact or approximate data, but the lesson teaches only the two-point exact-data formula. Its approximate datasets supply the candidate exponent and constant in advance, and no assessment asks the learner to estimate an exponent from noisy observations. Applying the exact two-point formula independently to noisy point pairs can produce unstable, mutually inconsistent estimates, so the promised approximate-data performance is neither defined nor assessed.
findings.resolution: Either narrow the objective to exact data and evaluation of supplied approximate-data candidates, or teach a defined approximate-data fitting procedure, including its fitting criterion, error-scale assumptions, residual checks, and domain limits, then add aligned practice and assessment requiring recovery of the exponent and constant from approximate data.

findings.finding: The claim includes attention to the observed domain, and the lesson repeatedly teaches interpolation-versus-extrapolation cautions. Direct inspection of source-nist-scatterplot and source-nist-transformations confirmed support for inspecting relationship structure, transformations, changing variation, residual assumptions, and the distinction between association and causation, but neither cited locator supports the observed-domain or extrapolation claim. The evidence trace is also incomplete because content/010-orientation.md states the residual-and-domain audit and content/030-worked-example.md applies residual and interpolation judgments, while claim-model-checking omits both scene IDs and their source notes omit the supporting model-checking sources. This leaves a central, assessment-rewarded model boundary only partially sourced and mapped.
findings.resolution: Add an authoritative source with an exact locator supporting interpolation, extrapolation, and observed-domain limits, or narrow the claim and assessed cautions to what the existing sources support. Map the retained model-checking claim to scene-orientation and scene-worked-example and update both same-scene source notes with every supporting source.

findings.finding: The statement that k has response units divided by input units raised to p is central to objective-parameterize-model and is explicitly scored by item-recover-power, but no claim in claims.json covers dimensional interpretation of k. The cited DLMF power/logarithm reference does not address physical dimensions or unit dependence. The lesson therefore assesses a material quantitative/model-boundary claim that is absent from the claim ledger and unsupported by the registered evidence.
findings.resolution: Add a scene-mapped claim for the dimensions and unit dependence of k, support it with an appropriate authoritative dimensional-analysis source and exact locator, and expose that mapping in the relevant source notes. State that the numerical value and units of k depend on the chosen units for x and y.

findings.finding: The displayed two-point formula for p says only that the points are positive. It is undefined when x2 equals x1 because log(x2/x1) is then zero; repeated-input observations also cannot identify p through that ratio.
findings.resolution: State explicitly that the two exact points must have distinct positive input values, so x2/x1 is positive and not equal to 1, and that the logarithm base must be valid and used consistently.

findings.finding: Independent recomputation confirmed the exact-data answer logic: the direct and inverse invariants, p = -2 scale-factor result, p = 2 and k = 4.5 recovery, base-10 log result p = 0.5 with k approximately 15.85, inverse-model products and residuals, and all worked/practice/transfer fitted values and residuals are numerically correct under the declared conventions.
findings.resolution: No change required to these numerical results or answer keys.

limitations: The authorized scope excluded site/, scripts/, workflows, and other lesson packs. Consequently, the graph outcome definition, prerequisite graph relationships, cross-course terminology ledger, quorum policy, and review JSON Schema were not independently inspected; identity, route relevance, terminology continuity, and automated structural validity remain unverified here.
Repository validation was not run because it would require the excluded scripts and site resources; no academic conclusion in this review relies on validator success.

The review directly checked all three registered NIST sources and found no human-only source. It did not perform empirical learner testing or formal statistical simulation; judgments about approximate-data fitting follow standard introductory mathematical and statistical model-identification principles.
